description: The Rt Hon Dame Diana Johnson DBE was appointed Minister of State at the Department for Work and Pensions on 6 September 2025. She was previously Minister of State in the Home Office between 8 July 2024 and 6 September 2025. Political career She was elected as the MP for Kingston upon Hull North and Cottingham at the general election in July 2024 and has represented Hull North, as Hull’s first female MP, since May 2005. Diana was Chair of the Home Affairs Select Committee from 2021 until 2024. Under the Labour government between 2005 and 2010 Diana was a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the Department of Children, Schools and Families and a government whip.  Between 2010 and 2015 Diana held a number of front bench roles including in the shadow Home Office team. She was named backbencher of the year in 2018 for her work to secure a public inquiry into the NHS contaminated blood scandal. Diana was appointed as a Commissioner on the Commonwealth War Graves Commission in 2019. Personal life In 2020 Diana received a Damehood for her political and charitable work.

details.body:
The Rt Hon Dame Diana Johnson DBE was appointed Minister of State at the Department for Work and Pensions on 6 September 2025.

She was previously Minister of State in the Home Office between 8 July 2024 and 6 September 2025.

Political career

She was elected as the MP for Kingston upon Hull North and Cottingham at the general election in July 2024 and has represented Hull North, as Hull’s first female MP, since May 2005.

Diana was Chair of the Home Affairs Select Committee from 2021 until 2024.

Under the Labour government between 2005 and 2010 Diana was a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the Department of Children, Schools and Families and a government whip. 

Between 2010 and 2015 Diana held a number of front bench roles including in the shadow Home Office team.

She was named backbencher of the year in 2018 for her work to secure a public inquiry into the NHS contaminated blood scandal.

Diana was appointed as a Commissioner on the Commonwealth War Graves Commission in 2019.

Personal life

In 2020 Diana received a Damehood for her political and charitable work.

She was previously Minister of State in the Home Office between 8 July 2024 and 6 September 2025.

She was elected as the MP for Kingston upon Hull North and Cottingham at the general election in July 2024 and has represented Hull North, as Hull’s first female MP, since May 2005.

Diana was Chair of the Home Affairs Select Committee from 2021 until 2024.

Under the Labour government between 2005 and 2010 Diana was a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the Department of Children, Schools and Families and a government whip. 

Between 2010 and 2015 Diana held a number of front bench roles including in the shadow Home Office team.

She was named backbencher of the year in 2018 for her work to secure a public inquiry into the NHS contaminated blood scandal.

Diana was appointed as a Commissioner on the Commonwealth War Graves Commission in 2019.

Personal life

In 2020 Diana received a Damehood for her political and charitable work.

details.body:
• Labour Market – including employer engagement 

  • Jobs and Careers Service reform/Jobcentre Plus, including post-19 careers 

  • Poverty 

  • Work and Health: Occupational Health and Statutory Sick Pay 

  • Locally led employment support 

  • Conditionality and Sanctions 

  • Childcare and Parental Employment 

  • In Work Progression (conditionality and careers) 

  • Devolution (Local) 

  • Disability Employment

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • policing standards and governance
  • neighbourhood policing
  • public order, major events, and civil contingencies
  • criminal justice system
  • Young Futures
  • Safer Streets
